name: Breast Cancer - Tamoxifen for Chemoprevention (No Diagnosis)
description: Tricky test case: finds patients on tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors who have NO breast cancer diagnosis. Unlike most Path C scenarios, breast cancer Path C has a clinically meaningful interpretation: tamoxifen and AIs are FDA-approved for breast cancer chemoprevention in high-risk women (BRCA1/2 mutation carriers, atypical hyperplasia, lobular carcinoma in situ). These patients receive endocrine therapy without an active breast cancer diagnosis. The Synthea module weights this path lightly (~15%) because chemoprevention is a much smaller population than treatment.
prompt: I want to find patients in our system who are taking tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors but don't have a formal breast cancer diagnosis on their problem list. These are typically high-risk patients on chemoprevention rather than treatment.

prompts.naive: Find patients on tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors without a breast cancer diagnosis.
prompts.expert: I need to identify the chemoprevention population: patients with MedicationRequest for tamoxifen (RxNorm 10324), anastrozole (199224), letrozole (200064), or exemestane (310261), who lack a Condition for breast cancer (SNOMED 254837009, 254838004, 408643008, 278054005, 713609000). These patients are typically BRCA1/2 carriers, have atypical hyperplasia, or have a strong family history but no active malignancy.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path C 'tricky' patients: have tamoxifen or AI but NO breast cancer Condition. The Synthea module weights this path lightly (~15%) — this is a smaller population than therapeutic use but clinically meaningful (BRCA carriers, atypical hyperplasia).
